purpose: Raw model tool_input from the extraction call, BEFORE validator filtering or curator review. Auditors read this to see what the model proposed. NOT a promotable manifest.

claims.title: MaxQuant peak matching error reaches up to 30% in 7.5 min, 150,000-peptide setup
claims.claim: MaxQuant's peak matching error rate rose sharply, reaching up to 30% in the 7.5 min, 150,000-peptide setup.

tolerances.source_span: MaxQuant's peak matching error rate rose sharply, reaching up to 30% in the 7.5 min, 150,000-peptide setup
tolerances.prose: In the 7.5-minute gradient with 150,000 peptides setup, MaxQuant's peak matching error rate reached up to 30%.

claims.title: PEAKS-XPro real FDR on 10k HLA-I dataset
claims.claim: PEAKS-XPro reported real FDRs of 1.8% (10k) and 1.15% (100k).

tolerances.source_span: PEAKS-XPro reported real FDRs of 1.8% (10k) and 1.15% (100k)
tolerances.prose: PEAKS-XPro reported a real FDR of 1.8% on the 10,000-peptide HLA-I dataset.

tolerances.source_span: PEAKS-XPro reported real FDRs of 1.8% (10k) and 1.15% (100k)
tolerances.prose: PEAKS-XPro reported a real FDR of 1.15% on the 100,000-peptide HLA-I dataset.

claims.title: FragPipe real FDR on HLA-I datasets
claims.claim: FragPipe achieved 0.91% and 1.16% on the same datasets (10k and 100k HLA-I).

tolerances.source_span: FragPipe achieved 0.91% and 1.16% on the same datasets
tolerances.prose: FragPipe achieved a real FDR of 0.91% on the 10,000-peptide HLA-I dataset.

tolerances.source_span: FragPipe achieved 0.91% and 1.16% on the same datasets
tolerances.prose: FragPipe achieved a real FDR of 1.16% on the 100,000-peptide HLA-I dataset.

claims.title: FragPipe identification rate in HLA-I 10k dataset
claims.claim: Of the 9,825 fragmented peptides in the 10k dataset, FragPipe identified 3,617 (36.8%).

tolerances.source_span: Of the 9,825 fragmented peptides in the 10k dataset, FragPipe identified 3,617 (36.8%)
tolerances.prose: FragPipe identified 36.8% (3,617 of 9,825) of fragmented peptides in the 10k HLA-I dataset.

claims.title: PEAKS-XPro identification rate in HLA-I 10k dataset
claims.claim: PEAKS-XPro identified 3,233 (32.9%) of the 9,825 fragmented peptides in the 10k dataset.

tolerances.prose: PEAKS-XPro identified 32.9% (3,233 of 9,825) of fragmented peptides in the 10k HLA-I dataset.

claims.title: FragPipe identification rate in HLA-I 100k dataset
claims.claim: In the 100k dataset, 92,047 peptides were fragmented, of which FragPipe recovered 28,108 (30.5%).

tolerances.prose: FragPipe recovered 30.5% (28,108 of 92,047) of fragmented peptides in the 100k HLA-I dataset.

claims.title: PEAKS-XPro identification rate in HLA-I 100k dataset
claims.claim: PEAKS-XPro recovered 27,182 (29.5%) of the 92,047 fragmented peptides in the 100k dataset.

tolerances.prose: PEAKS-XPro recovered 29.5% (27,182 of 92,047) of fragmented peptides in the 100k HLA-I dataset.

rejections.candidate_text: several dia-PASEF workflows control FDR near the nominal 1% threshold at stripped-sequence level but exhibit inflated true FDR (3–5%) when modified peptidoforms are considered
rejections.rationale: Subject is generic 'several dia-PASEF workflows', not a specific named tool. The 3-5% range is also not tied to a single subject with a comparator.

rejections.candidate_text: match-between-runs produced peak-matching errors of up to 30% under high-density conditions
rejections.rationale: Same finding is captured more specifically in body text and attributed to MaxQuant; abstract version is not tool-specific.

rejections.candidate_text: identifying a 0.65 site-probability cutoff as an optimal tradeoff between sensitivity and false localization
rejections.rationale: This is a recommended cutoff, not an empirical bound on a measured metric of a system.

rejections.candidate_text: DIA-NN v2.0, Spectronaut v20, and both tested FragPipe versions (v22 and v23) achieved a true FDR close to the nominal 1% threshold on ion level
rejections.rationale: 'Close to' is not a strict comparator with a specific numeric bound.

rejections.candidate_text: DIA-NN v1.9 ... converged below 1% at higher complexities
rejections.rationale: Conditional on 'higher complexities' (not precisely defined) and no specific measurement value tied to a clear comparator+subject in a way suitable for extraction.

rejections.candidate_text: we observed 3-4% for Spectronaut, up to 5% for DIA-NN v1.8, and 1.5-2% for DIA-NN v1.9 and v2.0
rejections.rationale: These are observed ranges rather than stated upper/lower bound comparators in the form required.

rejections.candidate_text: FragPipe was the only tool controlling FDR at the expected value of 1%
rejections.rationale: Qualitative statement without strict comparator and value bound on a specific metric.

rejections.candidate_text: in DIA-NN v1.9 and v2.0, the spurious oxidized form replaced the correct unmodified identification in 99% and 86% of cases
rejections.rationale: Reports point values without a comparator (no '<', '>', 'at least', 'at most' phrasing on these percentages).

rejections.candidate_text: all DIA-NN versions identified the largest number of precursors, up to 10% more than FragPipe
rejections.rationale: 'Up to 10% more' is a vague upper bound on a difference metric; not a cleanly tied claim of (metric, comparator, value, subject).

rejections.candidate_text: MBR contributed to 10–20% of identifications per run
rejections.rationale: Range value without a strict comparator.

rejections.candidate_text: Overall identification errors remained below 1% across both tools
rejections.rationale: Subject is 'both tools' generically; not tied to a single named subject for which the validator can match a source_span.

rejections.candidate_text: Each tool also contributed 10–15% unique identifications
